body:
{
  "title": "1:1 Seren Voss + Ora Yamamoto — 2026-03-10",
  "compiled_truth": "Weekly 1:1 between [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) (CTO) and [Ora Yamamoto](people/ora-yamamoto) (senior systems engineer) held on 2026-03-10. The meeting ran about 40 minutes over its scheduled half-hour slot, largely because the discussion around single-pass pipeline benchmarking turned into a deeper conversation about how [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) should handle regression testing as the codebase grows.\n\nSeren opened with a check-in on Ora's workload. Ora flagged that she's been splitting time between the deterministic compression layer refactor and helping onboard two new hires to the transmission module. She mentioned feeling stretched but manageable — \"not drowning, just treading.\" Seren acknowledged the load and said she'd look at rebalancing the onboarding responsibilities across the team.\n\nThe bulk of the meeting focused on a performance anomaly Ora had noticed during Friday's CI run. A particular test suite covering the authentication path showed ~8% throughput regression compared to the prior week's baseline. Ora hadn't yet isolated whether this was a genuine regression or an artifact of the new test harness configuration. Seren asked her to carve out a dedicated block on Wednesday to root-cause it and report back async. They also discussed whether to formalize a \"perf budget\" system — essentially a hard gate in CI that blocks merges if throughput drops beyond a defined threshold. Ora was enthusiastic; Seren was cautiously supportive but wanted to see a proposal before committing, noting that premature constraints could slow the team down during the current rapid iteration phase.\n\nThere was a brief tangent about team morale. Ora mentioned that a few people on the infra side feel disconnected from product-level decisions and would appreciate more visibility into roadmap priorities. Seren took the feedback seriously and said she'd bring it up at the next leadership sync.\n\nAction items: Ora to investigate the throughput regression by EOD Wednesday. Ora to draft a one-pager on the perf budget proposal by end of sprint. Seren to raise infra team visibility concerns with leadership. Next 1:1 scheduled for March 17th.",
  "timeline": [
    "- **2026-02-24** | [Ora Yamamoto](people/ora-yamamoto) joins [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) as senior systems engineer, assigned to deterministic compression layer.",
    "- **2026-03-06** | Ora flags CI throughput anomaly in Slack; initial investigation inconclusive.",
    "- **2026-03-10** | This 1:1 between [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) and Ora. Discussed regression testing, perf budgets, team morale.",
    "- **2026-03-12** | Ora isolates throughput regression to a misconfigured buffer size in the new test harness. Not a real regression.",
    "- **2026-03-17** | Follow-up 1:1 — Ora presents perf budget one-pager; Seren greenligths a two-week trial run.",
    "- **2026-04-02** | Perf budget gates go live in CI pipeline. First blocked merge triggers healthy debate on acceptable thresholds.",
    "- **2026-06-15** | Ora presents internal talk on \"Regression Detection in Single-Pass Pipelines\" at Anchor's monthly eng meetup.",
    "- **2026-09-22** | Seren promotes Ora to tech lead of the infrastructure reliability team."
  ]
}
